event.summary: Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.

acceptance.proof: The corpus records exact public source URLs, titles, publisher roles, access date, extracted claim identifiers, and an explicit no-source-code/no-implementation-input classification for the official LSL Stream Info API documentation and W3C XML 1.0 Fifth Edition.

Independently worded cases distinguish the documented top-level info role, documented core child roles, documented extended desc role, documented runtime/misc roles, XML legal-character domain, required character-data escaping, bounded input policy, and damaged-input classes without asserting exact serialized bytes or field order.

Every corpus case has a specification role plus separate oracle-observation and candidate-result roles; all observations and results remain explicitly not-observed with null evidence, and no STRM-000 result is promoted.

The crate, dependency and feature closure, source API, historical overlays, feature lock, runtime effects, and public/private boundary remain unchanged; the full source-only gate treats this as corpus tooling and specification evidence only.

document.commit_policy: Commit the accepted LSLC-001A public-documentation corpus separately from black-box oracle capture, XML implementation, exact serialization policy, protocol, discovery, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route public-documentation corpus intake and keep specification cases, black-box observations, candidate results, and implementation inputs separate.
Describe the corpus as specification and provenance evidence only, without presenting documented XML roles as implemented or observed compatibility.
Review whether this specification-versus-observation corpus boundary adds reusable intake guidance; avoid adding Rusty-LSL-specific fixture details when existing evidence-role rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the public/private and clean-source intake boundary for this corpus; retain the shared router unchanged if its existing Rusty LSL provenance rules already cover the unit.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Reading, copying, translating, adapting, summarizing, or structurally inspecting liblsl, rLSL, wrapper, application, test, build, generated, vendored, or protocol implementation source as an implementation input.

Capturing or inventing official endpoint output, asserting exact liblsl byte serialization, canonical field order, empty-element style, whitespace, numeric spelling, channel-format wire spelling, protocol-version behavior, XPath behavior, or any measured oracle or candidate result.

Implementing XML names, escaping, parsing, serialization, DOM mutation, schemas, queries, stream-info document assembly, descriptor conversion, runtime identity, discovery, networking, transport, clocks, buffering, inlets, outlets, providers, FFI, or native loading.
Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, crate API or behavior changes, generated protocol material, production liblsl integration, or language bindings.

Morphospace adapters, Manifold admission or stream authority, topology, permissions, commands, platform lifecycle, Quest or Hostess behavior, device validation, and any XML, protocol, wire, runtime, performance, recovery, or ecosystem compatibility claim.
document.objective: Create an independently authored, provenance-locked specification corpus for the documented LSL stream-info XML surface and the XML 1.0 character-data rules needed by later candidate work, while keeping all oracle and candidate results unobserved and implementing no parser, serializer, protocol, discovery, transport, runtime, or compatibility claim.
outputs: Public LSLC-001A stream-info document specification corpus with provenance records, bounded positive and damaged case roles, and null oracle/candidate evidence fields.
Corpus method documentation explaining source-role, specification, oracle, candidate, and later promotion boundaries without using external implementation source.
Deterministic LSLC-001A validation entrypoint wired into check_all, plus status updates that do not claim XML implementation, exact serialization, protocol, runtime, wire, or ecosystem support.
